mapping.a_element: square-lattice Ising spins encoding resident type
self-organised effective temperature T set by feedback with segregation

brief_justification: Both are lattice spin models sharing Ising vocabulary and the notion of an effective/self-organised temperature, but the governing machinery differs: paper A's segregation is an interaction-/energy-driven ordering (phase separation) with a temperature-feedback self-organisation, whereas paper B's defining feature is a trivial (non-interacting) equilibrium whose slow glassy dynamics arise purely from kinetic constraints on allowed transitions, so no role-for-role driving process corresponds.
